titleStmt.title: Letter from Captain E. Warner to Ludlow Beamish, Esq., 27 November, 1915
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: E. Warner

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: This is a letter from Captain E. Warner, the officer in charge of records for the no. 12 district, to Ludlow Beamish. The writer is returning a list of Connaught Rangers POWS on which he has been asked to indicate the counties to which the individual prisoners belong. Enclosed is a list of names (13 Pages) and an analysis/summary of the lists breaking them down by county for Connaught and Other Provinces.

msIdentifier.collection: Clonbrock Estate Papers, Collection List No. 54; Ms. 35,796/11/16

correspAction.placeName: Infantry Record Office, No. 12 District, Cork, Ireland.

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Infantry Record Office,
No 12 District,
Cork 27th. November 1915
Sir, I beg to return the attached list of Prisoners of
War of the Connaught Rangers which you handed me on Thursday
last (for insertion of the Counties to which they belong) com-
pleted as far as possible. Owing to the documents of these men being in different
rooms, viz:- Regulars, Special Reserve and New Army the lists
could not be completed sooner. All men belonging to the Counties of Galway and Mayo
are more readily recognized by the names of those Counties
being written in ink, the remainder as are shown in pencil. Those men whose names are not traced are marked accord-
ingly.
Yours obediently,
E. Warner. CAPTAIN
1/c Records, No.12 District.
Ludlow Beamish, Esq.,
Ashgrove,
QUEENSTOWN.
